facts.claim: R1 hostname R1
R1 ios_xe_version 16.12
R1 interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0 ip 192.168.0.1/24 (description LAN)
R1 interface Serial0/1/0 ip 10.0.0.1/30 (description Link to R2)
R1 interface Serial0/1/1 ip 10.0.0.10/30 (description Link to R3)
R1 OSPF process 1 router-id 1.1.1.1 area 0 interfaces: Serial0/1/1 (Point-to-Point, cost 49), GigabitEthernet0/0/0 (DR, cost 1)
R1 DHCP pool R1_LAN network 192.168.0.0/24 default-router 192.168.0.1 dns 8.8.8.8 domain lab-local (excluded 192.168.0.1-192.168.0.10)
R3 hostname R3
R3 ios_xe_version 16.12
R3 interface Loopback1 ip 203.0.113.1/32 (description Simulated Internet)
R3 interface Serial0/1/0 ip 10.0.0.9/30 (description Link to R1)
R3 interface Serial0/1/1 ip 10.0.0.6/30 (description Link to R2)
R3 OSPF process 1 router-id 3.3.3.3 area 0 interfaces: Loopback1 (Loopback, cost 1), Serial0/1/1 (Point-to-Point, cost 49), Serial0/1/0 (Point-to-Point, cost 50)
R3 sees OSPF neighbor 1.1.1.1 at 10.0.0.10 (adjacency full) on Serial0/1/0
R3 sees OSPF neighbor 2.2.2.2 at 10.0.0.5 (adjacency full) on Serial0/1/1
router2: device unreachable - All connection attempts failed

root_causes.type: Routing misconfiguration / forwarding loop or blackhole
root_causes.cause: R1 and R3 both have routes for 172.16.0.0/24 that point at each other (R1 forwards toward 10.0.0.9; R3 forwards toward 10.0.0.10), so packets for 172.16.0.12 will be forwarded between R1 and R3 with no valid onward path and are dropped.
evidence: R1 routing: ip-route-interface-forwarding-list entry for 172.16.0.0/24 fwd-list fwd=10.0.0.9 (from R1 running-config)
R3 routing: ip-route-interface-forwarding-list entry for 172.16.0.0/24 fwd-list fwd=10.0.0.10 (from R3 running-config)
R1 Serial0/1/1 ip 10.0.0.10 and R3 Serial0/1/0 ip 10.0.0.9 (interface IPs that match the reciprocal next-hops)

root_causes.type: Unreachable / uncollected config for intermediate router (router2)
root_causes.cause: Router2 (the intermediary between R1 and R3) could not be queried for running-config/OSPF (management connection failed). If the destination 172.16.0.0/24 is anchored on or learned from router2, its unreachability or missing advertisements would make 172.16.0.12 unreachable.
evidence: router2: device unreachable - All connection attempts failed (tool fetch errors for running-config, OSPF, DHCP)
R1 interface Serial0/1/0 configured with IP 10.0.0.1 description 'Link to R2' but R1's OSPF output shows no neighbor on Serial0/1/0 (neighbors=[] for that interface)
R3 sees OSPF neighbor 2.2.2.2 at 10.0.0.5 (adjacency full) on Serial0/1/1 — indicates router2 exists on R3 side but management/collection to router2 failed

missing_info: Which device/network is authoritative for 172.16.0.0/24 (which router/switch is 172.16.0.12 attached to)?
Provide router2's running-config / OSPF / routing table (management access currently failing) to confirm whether it owns or advertises 172.16.0.0/24.

data.intent_description: 172.16.0.12 is unreachable because router1 and router3 have reciprocal forwarding entries for 172.16.0.0/24 pointing at each other, creating a forwarding loop/blackhole; goal is to remove the erroneous entries so traffic can reach the authoritative router for 172.16.0.0/24.

plan.problem: 172.16.0.12 unreachable due to reciprocal ip-route-interface-forwarding-list entries on router1 and router3 pointing at each other (forwarding loop/blackhole).
plan.fix_summary: Remove the incorrect ip-route-interface-forwarding-list/static forwarding entries for 172.16.0.0/24 on router1 and router3 that point to each other. Operational risk: traffic to 172.16.0.0/24 may be dropped until the correct route is present or router2 advertises the network.

root_causes.type: Routing misconfiguration / forwarding loop or blackhole
root_causes.cause: R1 and R3 both have routes for 172.16.0.0/24 that point at each other (R1 forwards toward 10.0.0.9; R3 forwards toward 10.0.0.10), so packets for 172.16.0.12 will be forwarded between R1 and R3 with no valid onward path and are dropped.
evidence: R1 routing: ip-route-interface-forwarding-list entry for 172.16.0.0/24 fwd-list fwd=10.0.0.9 (from R1 running-config)
R3 routing: ip-route-interface-forwarding-list entry for 172.16.0.0/24 fwd-list fwd=10.0.0.10 (from R3 running-config)
R1 Serial0/1/1 ip 10.0.0.10 and R3 Serial0/1/0 ip 10.0.0.9 (interface IPs that match the reciprocal next-hops)

root_causes.type: Unreachable / uncollected config for intermediate router (router2)
root_causes.cause: Router2 (the intermediary between R1 and R3) could not be queried for running-config/OSPF (management connection failed). If the destination 172.16.0.0/24 is anchored on or learned from router2, its unreachability or missing advertisements would make 172.16.0.12 unreachable.

messages.content:
What was observed
- 172.16.0.12 was unreachable. R1 and R3 each had ip-route-interface-forwarding-list/static forwarding entries for 172.16.0.0/24 that pointed at the other router (R1 → 10.0.0.9, R3 → 10.0.0.10), creating a forwarding loop/blackhole. Router2 could not be queried by management.

Diagnosis (root cause)
- Reciprocal static/forwarding entries on R1 and R3 caused packets for 172.16.0.0/24 to be forwarded between them with no valid onward path (forwarding loop/blackhole). Router2’s configuration and whether it should be authoritative for 172.16.0.0/24 is unknown because it is unreachable.

Changes made
- Deleted static/forwarding entry for 172.16.0.0/24 from router1 (success).
- Deleted static/forwarding entry for 172.16.0.0/24 from router3 (success).

Verification
- Passed: Ping from R1 to 172.16.0.12 succeeded (5/5). R1 and R3 running-configs no longer contain ip-route-interface-forwarding-list entries for 172.16.0.0/24.
- Remaining: router2 still unreachable to management — need its running config/OSPF/routing to confirm the authoritative attachment for 172.16.0.0/24 and to ensure the route won’t be reintroduced. Recommend restoring management access to router2 and verifying which device advertises 172.16.0.0/24 (and re-enable correct advertisement if needed).

- Remaining: router2 still unreachable to management — need its running config/OSPF/routing to confirm the authoritative attachment for 172.16.0.0/24 and to ensure the route won’t be reintroduced. Recommend restoring management access to router2 and verifying which device advertises 172.16.0.0/24 (and re-enable correct advertisement if needed).
